articles.title: Screams of 'come on big b****cks' as thug soaked in petrol during caravan park hammer brawl
articles.description: Barry Parkes confronted Kevin Tucker over an assault on a neighbour, swinging a hammer at him before Tucker doused the grandfather in petrol as children watched on in terror at Halewood Caravan Park

articles.title: Rupee hits six-week high on offshore, exporter flows
articles.description: The Indian Rupee saw a remarkable resurgence on Thursday, hitting a six-week high after a period of weakness influenced by the Federal Reserve's strict monetary stance. This turnaround was fueled by increased dollar selling from foreign markets and robust exporter contributions. Furthermore, the recent downturn in oil prices offered additional support.

articles.title: India's current account deficit likely to rise to 2.2 pc of GDP in FY27 over elevated oil prices
articles.description: India's current account deficit likely to rise to 2.2 pc of GDP in FY27 over elevated oil prices - New Delhi, June 18 India's current account deficit is expected to rise to 2.2 per cent of gross ... Get Latest News on Business only on lokmattimes.com

articles.title: Strait of Hormuz Reopens After US-Iran MoU: Can the World’s Key Oil Chokepoint Ever Be Truly Safe?
articles.description: As the Strait of Hormuz reopens after the US–Iran MoU, global oil prices ease but doubts over long-term security, high-risk shipping routes and elevated insurance premiums persist, pushing countries to diversify energy sources and rethink dependence on the world’s most critical energy chokepoint.

articles.title: Israel Hit Iran’s Military. The US Forced A Deal. So Did Tehran Actually Win The War?
articles.description: US says it halted Iran’s nuclear ambitions. Israel says it weakened Iran’s military network. But after four months of war, soaring oil prices, Iran survived. But who actually won?

articles.title: Stocks to buy: Nagaraj Shetti recommends IRFC, Lloyds Enterprises shares to buy in the short-term
articles.description: Indian benchmark indices saw marginal gains as easing oil prices and improved geopolitical conditions countered US Federal Reserve concerns. Brent crude dropped 1.7% to $78.2 per barrel. Most sectoral indices were positive, but IT stocks fell 1.5% amid rate hike signals from the Fed.

articles.title: Systematix sees stronger outlook for cyclicals, turns overweight on banks
articles.description: Nikhil Khandelwal, Managing Director of Systematix Group, said the brokerage has turned more positive on the financial sector, as lower crude oil prices are expected to ease inflationary pressures, strengthen the rupee and pave the way for a softer interest rate cycle over the medium term.

articles.title: Indian rupee’s oil relief capped by RBI's FX book, interest payment hedges, bankers say
articles.description: The Indian rupee's rally on lower oil prices is likely to be constrained by the central bank's unwinding of its sizable FX forward book and hedging of interest obligations on foreign currency deposits raised by ​Indian banks.

articles.title: Crude falls below $80. So why aren't your petrol and diesel prices dropping?
articles.description: While crude prices have retreated sharply, analysts remain divided on how quickly global oil markets can return to normal

articles.title: World shares mixed following signing of US
articles.description: US futures were higher early Thursday, while oil prices fell.
